summary: postinstall hook exfiltrates developer git/GitHub identity to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js runs unconditionally and harvests developer-identifying data from the installer's machine, then POSTs it over HTTPS to npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app — a generic Google Cloud Run URL unrelated to the package's stated publisher domain (ai-sdk.guide). The script reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, the current project's.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI authentication config) to extract the developer's git email and GitHub CLI login/email. It additionally collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), and CI-provider environment variables. The README claims telemetry is limited to platform/Node/arch; the actual collection scope is materially broader and uniquely identifies the developer and their machine. Reading ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml is a deliberate probe of an installer-owned credential store and has no relationship to the package's advertised 'AI SDK helpers' purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
